evals.prompt: Create a CITATION.cff for this Node.js project. The repo has a package.json with name 'my-cli-tool', version '2.1.0', author 'Jane Smith <jane@example.com>', license 'MIT', and description 'A command-line tool for data processing'. Use --dry-run mode.
evals.expected_output: A valid CFF 1.2.0 CITATION.cff file displayed in dry-run mode with all required fields populated from the package.json metadata.
expectations: Output contains 'cff-version: 1.2.0' as the schema version
Output contains 'type: software' as the CFF type
Output contains 'title: "my-cli-tool"' or 'title: my-cli-tool' matching the package name
Output contains 'version: "2.1.0"' or 'version: 2.1.0' matching the package version
Author entry splits 'Jane Smith' into family-names: 'Smith' and given-names: 'Jane'
Output contains 'license: MIT' or 'license: "MIT"' as a valid SPDX identifier

evals.prompt: Create a CITATION.cff for this Python project. It has a pyproject.toml with [project] name = 'dataflow', version = '0.9.3', description = 'Streaming data pipeline framework', license = {text = 'Apache-2.0'}, and authors = [{name = 'Carlos Ruiz', email = 'carlos@example.org'}, {name = 'Wei Zhang'}]. Use --dry-run.
evals.expected_output: A valid CFF 1.2.0 CITATION.cff displayed in dry-run mode with metadata extracted from pyproject.toml, including both authors properly formatted.
expectations: Output contains 'cff-version: 1.2.0'
Output contains 'title: "dataflow"' or 'title: dataflow'
Output contains 'version: "0.9.3"' or 'version: 0.9.3'
Output lists two authors with correct family-names/given-names splits: Ruiz/Carlos and Zhang/Wei
Output contains 'license: Apache-2.0' or 'license: "Apache-2.0"'
Output includes a 'date-released' field in YYYY-MM-DD format

evals.prompt:
Validate this CITATION.cff file:

cff-version: 1.2.0
message: "Please cite this software."
title: "broken-project"
type: software
authors:
  - given-names: "Alice"
date-released: "2024-1-5"
version: 3.0
doi: "not-a-doi"
evals.expected_output: A validation report identifying multiple errors: the author entry is missing family-names, date-released has an invalid format, version should be a string not a number, and the DOI does not match the expected pattern.
expectations: Report identifies that the author entry is missing 'family-names' (each author must have family-names + given-names OR name)
Report identifies that 'date-released' value '2024-1-5' is invalid and should be '2024-01-05' (YYYY-MM-DD with zero-padding)
Report identifies that 'version' value 3.0 is a number and should be a quoted string
Report identifies that 'doi' value 'not-a-doi' does not match the DOI pattern 10.XXXX/...
Report ends with a Result line showing FAIL and a count of errors

evals.prompt:
Export the citation as BibTeX format from this CITATION.cff:

cff-version: 1.2.0
message: "If you use this software, please cite it as below."
title: "fast-parser"
type: software
authors:
  - family-names: "Kim"
    given-names: "Soo-Jin"
  - family-names: "Okafor"
    given-names: "Emeka"
version: "4.2.1"
date-released: "2025-08-10"
url: "https://github.com/example/fast-parser"
license: "BSD-3-Clause"
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.9876543"
evals.expected_output: A BibTeX @software entry with correct field mappings, including both authors in 'Family, Given' format, the DOI, version, date, URL, and a properly formed BibTeX key.
expectations: Output contains '@software{' indicating the correct BibTeX entry type
Output contains 'author' field with both authors in 'Family, Given' format: 'Kim, Soo-Jin' and 'Okafor, Emeka'
BibTeX key includes 'kim' (first author family name lowercase) and '2025' (year)
Output contains 'doi = {10.5281/zenodo.9876543}' or equivalent DOI field
Output contains 'version = {4.2.1}' or equivalent version field
Output contains 'license = {BSD-3-Clause}' or equivalent license field

evals.prompt:
Export this CITATION.cff as APA format:

cff-version: 1.2.0
message: "If you use this software, please cite it as below."
title: "neural-toolkit"
type: software
authors:
  - family-names: "Patel"
    given-names: "Anita"
  - family-names: "Johansson"
    given-names: "Erik"
  - family-names: "Costa"
    given-names: "Maria"
version: "1.5.0"
date-released: "2025-03-22"
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.1111111"
evals.expected_output: An APA-formatted citation string with authors in 'Family, Initial.' format, an ampersand before the last author, year in parentheses, title, version, [Computer software] designator, and DOI URL.
expectations: Output contains authors formatted as 'Patel, A.' and 'Johansson, E.' and 'Costa, M.' with initials (not full given names)
Output uses '&' or 'and' before the last author (Costa, M.) per APA style
Output contains '(2025)' as the year in parentheses
Output contains '(Version 1.5.0)' after the title
Output contains '[Computer software]' type designator
Output contains the DOI as a URL: 'https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.1111111'

evals.prompt: Sync the version in my CITATION.cff from the package manifest. My CITATION.cff has version '1.0.0' and date-released '2024-06-01', but my package.json has version '1.3.0'. Use --dry-run to show what would change.
evals.expected_output: A diff/comparison showing the version update from 1.0.0 to 1.3.0 and the date-released update to today's date, displayed in dry-run mode without writing.
expectations: Output shows the version changing from '1.0.0' to '1.3.0'
Output shows the date-released being updated to today's date (or a current date in YYYY-MM-DD format)
Output explicitly states this is a dry-run and changes have NOT been written to disk
Output presents changes in a diff or comparison table format showing old vs new values

evals.prompt:
Validate this CITATION.cff file and confirm it is correct:

cff-version: 1.2.0
message: "If you use this software, please cite it as below."
title: "good-project"
type: software
authors:
  - family-names: "Mueller"
    given-names: "Hans"
    orcid: "https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1234-5678"
version: "2.0.0"
date-released: "2025-01-15"
url: "https://github.com/example/good-project"
repository-code: "https://github.com/example/good-project"
license: "MIT"
keywords:
  - testing
  - validation
evals.expected_output: A validation report with zero errors, confirming the file is valid CFF 1.2.0. May include informational notes about DOI not being set.
expectations: Report result is PASS (not FAIL) with 0 errors
Report does not flag any required fields as missing
Report correctly identifies that 1 author is listed
Report notes that ORCID is present and valid for the author (format matches https://orcid.org/XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX)
Report identifies license 'MIT' as a valid SPDX identifier
